notice: This manifest documents public claims and boundaries. The interactive challenges are explanatory simulations, not repository execution or certification.

projects.name: RGIT Rozgar
projects.statement: A governed campus resource platform for accommodation, resale, academic resources, food services, and nearby healthcare.
boundaries: Adoption figures published only when measured
Repository CI must be interpreted separately from product availability

challenges.label: Apply failed CI
challenges.effect: Repository confidence weakens; the deployed-product claim is not automatically erased.

projects.name: Kairo-Phantom
projects.statement: A local-first desktop agent with human confirmation and signed, hash-chained action records inspectable by a separately runnable verifier.
evidence: Published result: 12 passed and 0 outbound connections detected

challenges.label: Alter receipt byte
challenges.effect: Downstream receipt seals become invalid in this explanatory model.

challenges.label: Disconnect verifier
challenges.effect: The claim remains authored but loses separately runnable checking.

challenges.label: Introduce egress
challenges.effect: The declared zero-egress claim fractures for the challenged surface.

projects.name: Project X-Ray India
projects.statement: Source-linked public-infrastructure claims organized for human investigation and explicit review.
boundaries: Sources are claims and records, not automatic proof

challenges.label: Remove a source
challenges.effect: Dependent conclusions become unsupported and fade.

projects.name: Proving Grounds
projects.statement: Behavioral claims tested across base and head revisions with replayable evidence capsules.
boundaries: Not a replacement for human review

challenges.label: Substitute vacuous test
challenges.effect: Tension exposes that the oracle no longer checks the behavioral claim.

challenges.label: Erase revision delta
challenges.effect: The claimed behavioral change loses its differential support.
